title: Fermi Arcs Terminate At Projected Weyl Nodes
summary: The endpoints of a Fermi arc in the surface Brillouin zone are fixed by the projections of bulk Weyl nodes that source the changing topological data across momentum slices.
assumptions: Projected Weyl nodes remain distinguishable on the boundary.
regime: Boundary spectrum of a Weyl semimetal.
scope: Lecture One qualitative bulk-boundary statement for Fermi arcs.

source_anchors.notes: Lecture One identifies Fermi arcs as boundary signatures tied to bulk Weyl nodes.

representation: Boundary claim linking arc endpoints to projected bulk topology.
failure_modes: Surface reconstructions or coincident projections can obscure the simple arc picture.
retrieval_hints: Use when a query asks how to read bulk Weyl nodes from boundary spectra.
